Hearing On Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' Request For Stay
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Hearing On Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' Request For Stay.

Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' request for a stay of this action is granted until April 8, 2019. If Ms. Karnazes seeks a further stay of the action at that time, she is ordered by April 8, 2019 to serve Defendants' counsel with a declaration attaching medical records that provide evidentiary support for that request. Such declaration shall be served by hand and email on opposing counsel, and a courtesy copy must be provided to Department 302, specifying that it is for the Court's viewing and not for filing. If Defendants wish to object to the sufficiency of Ms. Karnazes's declaration and medical records, they must do so via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org by April 10, 2019 with a copy to Ms. Karnazes. If Defendants object, both parties must appear for an ex parte hearing on April 11, 2019 at 11 a.m. If Ms. Karnazes does not timely serve and lodge an adequate declaration, the Court will hold a hearing on Defendants' pending motions on April 30, 2019 at 9:30 a.m.

On July 20, 2018 and January 14, 2019, the Court issued similar orders. Ms. Karnazes' submissions complied minimally with those orders. The Court admonishes Ms. Karnazes that any submission in support of a further request for a stay must attach medical records that substantiate in detail any contention that her surgeries and medical conditions will continue to preclude her from participating in this lawsuit after April 8, 2019. The Court observes that Ms. Karnazes' medical issues did not prevent her from filing in this action numerous case management statements, responses to orders to show cause, an amended complaint, a request for entry of default, statements of damages, a response to another defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint, and several ex parte applications accompanied by lengthy declarations seeking stays of the action. In addition, the Court takes judicial notice of its own records, which establish that since this action was filed in October 2014, Ms. Karnazes has filed no fewer than nine additional lawsuits in this Court alone, eight of which were filed after her medical problems supposedly began in May 2015. The Court will take these activities into account in determining whether Ms. Karnazes has met her burden to justify any further stay of the litigation. If Ms. Karnazes seeks a further stay, her declaration shall also inform Defendants and the Court of any efforts she has made to retain counsel to represent her in this action. (See Karnazes Decl. (June 12, 2018) ["I need to retain an attorney to represent me in this matter and to file and serve an amended complaint
with the help of an attorney. I do not want to represent myself in this case."].) Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BANFIELD PET HOSPITAL's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES

Defendant Banfield Pet Hospital's demurrer to the request for punitive damages in the first amended complaint is treated as a motion to strike the request for punitive damages and, as so treated, is granted. Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' request for punitive damages is stricken because she is required per CCP 425.13 to seek leave of court to allege a request for punitive damages and it is also too late for her to do so per that statute.

Counsel for Banfield Pet Hospital is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
